title: Garlicky Molten Canadian Cheese Poutine
description: A midnight craving Canadian dish that combines garlic, saffron, and shio-cure flavors with melted cheese and crispy fries.

ingredients.name: Saffron
ingredients.notes: threads, soaked in 1 tsp hot water

instructions: Cut the potatoes into 1/2-inch thick rounds and soak in cold water for at least 30 minutes to remove excess starch.
Drain the potatoes and pat dry with paper towels.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot to 375°F (190°C) and fry the potatoes in batches until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per batch.
Drain the fried potatoes on paper towels and set aside.
In a separate saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat and cook the minced garlic until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the flour to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Slowly pour in the beef broth, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the mascarpone cheese until melted and smooth.
Add the shredded cheddar cheese and stir until melted and well combined.
Season the cheese sauce with shio-cure salt and soaked saffron threads.
To assemble the poutine, place a portion of fries in a bowl, top with a ladle of the cheese sauce, and garnish with fresh thyme sprigs.
